input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:02:49.755Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` takes `Light` and `Dark` values and automatically switches whenever the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme<T>()`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
// Extension method on any BindableObject
myLabel.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark

myPage.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

---

## Key points

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Automatic** | Both approaches react to OS theme changes at runtime — no manual listener required. |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For reuse, define colors in `App.xaml` as `<Color>` resources and reference them inside `AppThemeBinding` with `{StaticResource}`. |
| **Detecting current theme** | `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified`. |
| **Override user preference** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` to force a theme regardless of system setting. |
